home.label: Auditra Home
home.description: Learn how to use the app

validation.unsupportedFormat: Unsupported audio format. Supported formats: WAV, MP3, M4A
validation.fileTooLarge: The file is too large. Maximum size is 2GB

errors.uploadFailed: Failed to upload the file. Please try again.
errors.onlyOneFile: You can only upload one file at a time.
errors.onlyOneFileAtATime: A file is currently being analyzed. Please remove it before uploading another.

home.title: Quick Application Guide
home.description: Start with evidence upload and proceed through metadata, patterns, and AI analysis to complete a consistent forensic evaluation.
home.intro: Auditra is an audio forensic analysis application designed to detect whether an audio file was generated by artificial intelligence. Follow this quick guide to understand how to use each module and obtain solid results.
home.noticeText: To understand the analysis process better, follow this order: Upload Audio -> Metadata -> Patterns -> AI Analysis.

upload.title: Upload the Evidence
upload.description: Upload a single file (WAV, MP3, or M4A). Auditra does not alter the original file; it always works on a copy to ensure evidence integrity.

metadata.title: Review Metadata
metadata.description: Inspect all types of metadata to detect traces that AI may have left behind. Auditra uses standard forensic tools such as Mutagen, Wave, ExifTool, and FFprobe to extract information.

patterns.title: Analyze Binary Patterns
patterns.description: Visualize self-similarity at the data (byte) level graphically, as well as edge padding, to identify synthesis artifacts.

analysis.title: Get the Final Conclusion
analysis.description: Based on the results of the analyses from the previous steps, Auditra generates a final assessment of possible AI generation.

audioIngestion.title: Upload Audio Evidence
audioIngestion.description: Securely upload and catalog audio evidence.
audioIngestion.dragDrop: Drag and drop audio files here
audioIngestion.uploadInfo: You will be able to analyze the file to detect whether it contains AI-generated audio. Only one file can be uploaded at a time. Once uploaded, you can remove it to upload another. All evidence analysis is performed on a copy of the original file, which remains unchanged to ensure chain of custody integrity.
audioIngestion.highNoiseText: Visualizes self-similarity patterns in the byte stream and detects padding or null sequences at the beginning and end offsets, revealing the fingerprint of synthesis engines.
audioIngestion.batchText: Inspects internal records, encoding libraries, and bitrate discrepancies. Identifies traces of third-party software and anomalies in file architecture through multi-library analysis.

metadata.title: Metadata Analysis
metadata.description: This module extracts complete technical information from your file using advanced forensic tools such as Mutagen, Wave, ExifTool, and FFprobe. View all extracted metadata to identify traces of manipulation, specific software, bitrate inconsistencies, and anomalies in the file architecture that may indicate AI generation or alteration.
metadata.noFileMessage: Upload a file in the 'audio upload section' first.

patterns.title: Pattern Analysis
patterns.description: Visualize the internal structure of the audio file as a bitmap. Each pixel represents the value of a byte, displayed in grayscale. Detect anomalous patterns, discontinuities, and padding sequences that may indicate AI generation or manipulation. Adjust the resolution to analyze at different scales.
patterns.noFileMessage: Upload a file in the 'audio upload section' first.
patterns.invalidWidth: Width must be between 128 and 2048 bytes
patterns.autosimilarityDesc: Each pixel represents the value of a byte in the file: darker colors are low values (0x00), lighter colors are high values (0xFF). Self-similarity reveals repetition patterns in the data. Natural audio shows gradual and varied transitions, while AI-generated synthetic audio tends to show more regular, repetitive patterns or uniform blocks that reveal its artificial processing.
patterns.paddingDesc: Examines the first and last 1024 bytes of the file. Legitimate audio contains data until the very end. Suspicious audio often contains padding (repeated bytes such as 0x00 or 0xFF) at the edges, indicating re-encoding, incomplete export, or manipulation by speech synthesis software.

analysis.title: AI Detection Analysis
analysis.description: Consolidated analysis that integrates metadata and patterns to determine whether the audio was generated by artificial intelligence. Uses advanced indicators based on encoding fingerprints, audio configuration, codecs, file format, size, and padding patterns.
analysis.noFileMessage: Upload a file in the 'audio upload section' first.
analysis.emptyMessage: Click "Analyze for AI" to start the analysis

encoding_library.reasoning: FFmpeg/Lavf is common software in audio synthesis and AI tools

mono_audio.reasoning: Mono audio is less common in natural recordings, frequent in AI synthesis

padding_pattern.reasoning: Excessive repetitive bytes padding is a strong indicator of automated AI audio processing

timestamp_consistency.reasoning_identical: Creation and modification timestamps are identical
timestamp_consistency.reasoning_reversed: Creation timestamp is after modification timestamp

codec_consistency.reasoning_mismatch: Codec inconsistent with format may indicate manipulation or re-encoding
codec_consistency.reasoning_error: Error: format or codec not available for verification

file_size.reasoning_ok: File size consistent with bitrate and duration
file_size.reasoning_high_deviation: File size deviated >30% from expected - HIGH risk
file_size.reasoning_medium_deviation: File size deviated 20-30% from expected - MEDIUM risk
file_size.reasoning_low_deviation: File size deviated <20% from expected - LOW risk

self_similarity.reasoning: Highly repetitive byte patterns indicate artificial processing and AI generation

conclusions.no_indicators: No indicators of AI generation were detected. This audio appears to be an authentic recording.
conclusions.low_risk: The analysis indicates low risk of AI generation. Although some minor indicators were detected, there is no conclusive evidence of artificial processing.
conclusions.medium_risk: The analysis indicates medium risk of AI generation. Multiple suspicious indicators were detected. A deeper analysis is recommended.
conclusions.high_risk_critical: The analysis indicates high risk of AI generation. Critical indicators were detected. This audio was very likely generated by synthesis or AI tools.
conclusions.high_risk_multiple: The analysis indicates high risk of AI generation. Multiple indicators suggest artificial processing.

recommendations.padding_pattern: The detected  repetitive bytes padding is a strong indicator of automated processing. Consider analyzing files from the same source to confirm a pattern.
recommendations.timestamp_consistency: A timestamp inconsistency was detected. The file may have been manipulated or re-encoded. Verify the integrity of the source.
recommendations.encoding_library: FFmpeg/Lavf was detected as the encoding library. While it's a legitimate tool, it's also common in audio synthesis tools. Analyze alongside other indicators.
recommendations.mono_audio: Mono audio is less common in natural recordings. Typical in voice synthesis or automated processing.
recommendations.codec_consistency: A mismatch between codec and file format was detected. This may indicate manipulation or re-encoding of the original audio.
recommendations.file_size: The file size shows a significant deviation from the expected bitrate and duration. This may indicate re-encoding or additional compression.
recommendations.multiple_indicators: Multiple indicators were detected. We recommend getting a second opinion through analysis with other deepfake detection tools.
recommendations.no_indicators: The audio does not show typical indicators of AI generation. However, AI techniques are constantly evolving.
